title: Comforting Roasted Norwegian Date Hash
description: A light and comforting Norwegian dish featuring roasted pitted dates, ferment-kimchi, and halal-friendly ingredients, perfect for a hangover cure.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the diced potatoes and onions with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potato and onion mixture on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes.
While the potatoes are roasting, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ferment-kimchi to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes, until heated through.
After the potatoes have roasted for 20 minutes, add the pitted dates to the baking sheet and continue to roast for an additional 10 minutes.
To assemble the hash, add the roasted potato and date mixture to the skillet with the kimchi and garlic, and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the ingredients are well combined and heated through.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
